result.text:
unfortunately, crypto prime time has already passed, and the market is indeed becoming more “serious,” if we can put it that way

obviously, this will bring much more liquidity into the market, but it’s also obvious (as you pointed out) that it could potntially kill crypto by reintroducing the very consequences we were trying to escaspe through blockchain technology in the first place

but really, we have to stand with MetaLeX and simply not allow this to happen, even though I don’t have much faith that we’ll actually succeed

result.text: One of the saddest aspects of the increasing civil unrest in the US is that leadership in both ruling political parties could end this if they wanted but they specifically choose not to in order to cement power and control for personal gain. The vast majority of the country agrees on a common sense approach that involves policing criminals and not shooting American protesters but the political ruling class prefers a divided society where individuals are pitted against each other, rather than against the system. This is no longer left vs. right, but rather class warfare - elite/wealthy/powerful vs. citizens. Over time this is becoming better understood by the general public and the populist heat will continue to rise and be increasingly directed towards politicians and leadership who own accountability for outcomes as the elected officials in charge.

result.text:
In case you are curious how John Daghita (Lick) was able to steal $40M+ from US government seizure addresses. 

John’s dad owns CMDSS, which currently has an active IT government contract in Virginia. 

CMMDS was awarded a contract to assist the USMS in managing/disposing of seized/forfeited crypto assets. 

It still remains unclear at this point how John obtained access from his dad.
